SHORT TITLE: sSE NUMBER:
Westerfield v. USF CGC 03-417577
ANSWER-Contract Page two
4. () AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES
Defendant alleges the following additional reasons that ptaintiff is not entitled to recover anything:
1.Statute of Limitations as to all causes of action
2.Her Conduct is not actionable as to all causes of action because it was protected
activity under the Fair Employment Housing Act.
3.Cross-Complainant fails to state a cause ‘of action for any of its allegations or
causes of action
4.The Crime Fraud defense because USF sought to cover up its illegal conduct (all causes ,
5.Post-Termination Conduct cannot serve as the basis for a complaint for breach of
contract seeking injunctive or declaratory relief.
6.The doctrine of equitable estoppel applies to all causes of action.
7.Cross-complainant lacks standing to assert a cause of action relating to third party
privacy rights in the inten. tort, conversion and declaratory relief causes of action.
8.The Cross-Complaint is void as violating public policy with respect to all causes of
action.
9.The Crose-Claim is barred in its entirety as USF has already sought relief by
asserting the after acquired evidence doctrine
1.Statute of frauds as to the breach of contract causes of action for injunctive and
declaratory relief. :
11.The purpose of the contract was illegal in that it sought to prevent Ms.
Westerfield from establishing pretext and improper motive in her discrimination case.
12.The information that is allegedly confidential is not confidential and therefore
no basis exists for the crossa-complaint in its entirety.
(-) Continued on Attachment 4.
5. [K] Other:
Cross-Defendant states that she discovered illegal conduct by defendant in
that it reported false numbers to the US Government. She complained about
this and the false reasons for the elimination of her position. Shortly
after her complaint, she was fired. The information that Cross-Complainant
seeks to have returned is necessary to establish the illegal conduct and to
establish Cross-Complainant's pretextual, retaliatory termination of her
and of its discriminatory animus. Therefore, her conduct cannot serve as
the basis for any cross-complaint against her. Moreover, she never agreed
to keep information about the number and types of students with
disabilities confidential as this information is not confidential and must
be reported to the federal government.
6. DEFENDANT PRAYS
a. that plaintiff take nothing.
b. (X) for costs of suit.
c. KJ other (specify):
For Statutory Attorney's Fees pursuant to the Fair
Employment Housing Act.
For any and all such relief as the court deems appropriate.
